results.question: Mr. and Mrs. Mustard have six daughters and each daughter has one brother. But there are only 9 people in the family, how is that possible?
results.correct_answer: Each daughter shares the same brother.
results.model_answer: Each daughter shares the same brother.

results.question: A woman shoots her husband. Then she holds him underwater for over 5 minutes. Finally, she hangs him. But 5 minutes later, they both go out and enjoy a wonderful dinner together. How can this be?
results.correct_answer: The woman was a photographer. She shot a picture of her husband, developed it, and hung it up to dry.
results.model_answer: The woman was a photographer. She shot a picture of her husband, developed it, and hung it up to dry.

results.question: Samuel was out for a walk when it started to rain. He did not have an umbrella and he wasn't wearing a hat. His clothes were soaked, yet not a single hair on his head got wet. How could this happen?
results.correct_answer: This man is bald.

results.question: Five apples are in a basket. How do you divide them among five girls so that each girl gets an apple, but one apple remains in the basket?
results.correct_answer: Give the fifth girl her apple in the basket.
results.model_answer: Give the fifth girl her apple in the basket.

results.question: Tom and his younger sister were fighting. Their mother was tired of the fighting and decided to punish them by making them stand on the same piece of newspaper in such a way that they couldn't touch each other. How did she accomplish this?
results.correct_answer: Tom's mother slid a newspaper under a door, each sibling standing on each side.
results.model_answer: Tom's mother slid a newspaper under a door, each sibling standing on each side.

results.question:
A horse is tied to a five-meter rope in front of an old saloon. Ten meters behind the horse is a bale of hay. Without breaking his rope, the horse is able to eat the hay whenever he chooses.
How is this possible?
results.correct_answer: The rope is not tied to anything else.
results.model_answer: The rope is not tied to anything else.

results.question: All of Mrs. Smith pets are dogs except one, and all her pets are cats except one. But Mrs.Smith only has 2 pets, how is that possible?
results.correct_answer: Mrs. Smith has one cat and one dog.
results.model_answer: Mrs. Smith has one cat and one dog.

results.question: The 22nd and 24th presidents of the United States of America had the same parents, but were not brothers. How can this be possible?
results.correct_answer: They were the same man.

results.question: There were eight girls going to school. They were sharing one umbrella. How come all the girls did not get wet?
results.correct_answer: Because it was not raining.

results.question: A black dog stands in the middle of an intersection in a town painted black. None of the street lights are working due to a power failure caused by a storm. A car with two broken headlights drives towards the dog but turns in time to avoid hitting him. How could the driver have seen the dog in time?
results.correct_answer: It was during the daylight hours.
results.model_answer: The driver could have relied on their sense of hearing to detect the presence of the dog.

results.question: On a fine sunny day a ship was in the harbor. All of a sudden the ship began to sink. There was no storm and nothing wrong with the ship yet it sank right in front of the spectators eyes. What caused the ship to sink?
results.correct_answer: It was a Submarine.

results.question: A man who lived on the top floor of a twenty story building had to go up and down daily for work, and of course, for food and the other necessities. On most days he could only ride the elevator to the fifteenth story, and he would have to walk the rest of the way. When it rained, however, he could ride all the way up to the twentieth story. Why?
results.correct_answer: The man wasn't tall enough to reach the button for the 20th floor. He could only reach the 15th story button on the elevator. When it rained, he brought his umbrella, and used it to press the 20th story button on the elevator.
results.model_answer: The man wasn't tall enough to reach the button for the 20th floor. He could only reach the 15th story button on the elevator. When it rained, he brought his umbrella, and used it to press the 20th story button on the elevator.

results.question: Even if they are starving, natives living in the Arctic will never eat a penguin's egg. Why not?
results.correct_answer: Penguins only live in Antarctica.

results.question: There once was an evil wizard. He took 3 woman from their homes and turned them into rose bushes that looked exactly alike. He put them in his garden. One of the woman had a husband and children and begged the wizard to let her see them. He agreed. At night, he brought the woman to her house. In the morning he came and took her home. One day the husband decided to go rescue her. So he stuck into the wizard's garden. He looked and looked at the 3 identical rose bushes trying to figure out which could be his wife. Suddenly, he knew the answer and he took his wife home. How did he know which rose bush was his wife?
results.correct_answer: The wizard brought the rosebush to her home at night and returned her to the garden in the morning. Therefore, she was the only plant without dew.
results.model_answer: The wizard brought the rosebush to her home at night and returned her to the garden in the morning. Therefore, she was the only plant without dew.

results.question: There are 3 apples for 2 sons and 2 fathers to eat. Each of them get their own apple. How is it numerically possible?
results.correct_answer: They are one son, one father and one grandfather.
results.model_answer: They are one son, one father and one grandfather.

results.question: A man moved into a new house and decided he was tired after the journey and turned off all the lights and went to bed. The next morning he killed 365 people, he does not sleep walk he slept like a baby. How could this be?
results.correct_answer: He lived in a light house.
results.model_answer: The man could be a calendar maker. He accidentally excluding February 29th on a leap year's calendar before sleep. This "killed" the day for 365 people.

results.question: Joe and Sally were sitting in their family room one night. While Joe was watching T.V, his wife Sally was reading. All of a sudden the power went out and Joe decided to go to bed, but Sally kept on reading. With no use of artificial light, Sally kept on reading. How?
results.correct_answer: Sally was blind, she was reading a book by Braille.
results.model_answer: Sally was blind, she was reading a book by Braille.

results.question: Two men were playing tennis. They played five sets and each man won three sets. How can this be possible?
results.correct_answer: The two men were partners playing doubles.
results.model_answer: The two men were partners playing doubles.

results.question: A simple peasant bought a number of camels and is taking them to his farm. As he rides contentedly along he counts them - he counts 29. He was sure he had bought 30 camel, so in alarm he jumps off his camel and counts the camels again. To his delight there are 30. Half an hour later, he counts his camels and once more there are just 29. Confused, he climbs off his camel and counts again. Once more there are 30. Can you explain?
results.correct_answer: When he is on the camel he omits to count it.
results.model_answer: When he is on the camel he omits to count it.

results.question: One day a little boy named John went over to his parents and said that he wanted to shoot people and blow them up. His parents were really proud of his career choice. keep in mind that his parents were not very violent people. Why?
results.correct_answer: John wanted to be a photographer.
results.model_answer: John wanted to be a photographer.

results.question: Brad started through the dirty sort-shared window on the 22nd floor of the office tower. Overcome with depression he slid the window open and jumped through it. It was a sheer drop outside the building to the ground. Miraculously after he landed he was completely uninjured. Since there was nothing to cushion his fall or slow his descent, how could he have survived the fall?
results.correct_answer: Brad was so sick and tired of window washing, he opened the window and jumped inside.
results.model_answer: Brad was so sick and tired of window washing, he opened the window and jumped inside.

results.question: A horse jumps over a tower and the tower disappears. It's not an illusion or a dream. So where could this happen?
results.correct_answer: On a chessboard.

results.question: Dorothy has never expressed suicidal thoughts, but whenever she experiences feelings of sadness or depression, she makes arrangements to travel to a very high spot that she has visited on numerous occasions. Once there, she proceeds to jump from that great height. The fascinating fact though is she has never been injured from this leap, and in fact, she tells everyone that she feels much better afterward. If she’s not suicidal, then what is going on here, and why has she never been injured, or even worse, died from her risky actions?
results.correct_answer: Dorothy is a sky jumper.

results.question: A certain large animal lives happily and thrives here on Earth. One day, every single one of these writers is wiped out by a mysterious disease that affects only this particular animal. There are none left anywhere on earth -- they are all gone. About a year or so later, they begin to reappear on Earth again. How can this be?
results.correct_answer: The animal is the Mule. Since all Mules are born sterile, you can only get a Mule by crossing a donkey with a horse.

results.question: Four men were in a boat on the lake. The boat turns over, and all four men sink to the bottom of the lake, yet not a single man got wet! Why?
results.correct_answer: Because they were all married and not single.
results.model_answer: Because they were all married and not single.

results.question: A horse travels a certain distance each day. Strangely enough, two of its legs travel 30 miles each day and the other two legs travel nearly 31 miles. It would seem that two of the horse's legs must be one mile ahead of the other two legs, but of course, this can't be true, since the horse is normal. How is this possible?
results.correct_answer: The horse operates a mill and travels in a circular clockwise direction. The two outside legs will travel a greater distance than the two inside legs.
results.model_answer: The horse operates a mill and travels in a circular clockwise direction. The two outside legs will travel a greater distance than the two inside legs.

results.question: A man walking along a railroad track sees a train laundering at high speed towards him. Instead of immediately jumping off the track, he charges directly at the train for about ten feet and only then gets off the track. Why?
results.correct_answer: The man was on a bridge when he first saw the train so he couldn't jump off the track immediately.

results.question: There were 5 men traveling down a road and it started to rain and 4 men sped up, the 5th did not, but they all arrived at the same place at the same time but all of them were wet besides the 5th. How is that possible?
results.correct_answer: He was dead and in a coffin.

results.question: Three men sitting in a small motorboat one meter from the shoreline. The first is afraid of water, the second is afraid of drowning, and the third is afraid of sharks. The boat's motor is not operational and there is nothing to row with. How do they get to the shoreline?
results.correct_answer: They can just walk onto its as the boat is one-meter inland.
results.model_answer: They can just walk onto its as the boat is one-meter inland.

results.question: A father and son were in a car accident where the father was killed. The ambulance brought the son to the hospital. He needed immediate surgery. In the operating room, a doctor came in and looked at the little boy and said I can't operate on him he is my son. Why?
results.correct_answer: Because the doctor is the mother of the son.

results.question: A beggar's brother died, but the man who died had no brother. How could that be?
results.correct_answer: The beggar was the man's sister.
results.model_answer: The beggar was the man's sister.

results.question: I dig out tiny caves and store gold and silver in them. I also build bridges of silver and make crowns of gold. They are the smallest you could imagine. Sooner or later everybody needs my help, yet many people are afraid to let me help them. Why?
results.correct_answer: I am a dentist.

results.question: How could a cowboy ride into town on Friday, stay two days, and ride out on Wednesday?
results.correct_answer: His horse is named Wednesday.

results.question: There are three doors. In each door is a way to die. The first door has fire and lava. The second door has lions that haven't eaten in 5 years. In the third door, there is a 1,000-foot drop into an alligator. Which door will you likely survive?
results.correct_answer: The second door with the lions. Since they haven't eaten in 5 years they will have died.
results.model_answer: The second door with the lions. Since they haven't eaten in 5 years they will have died.

results.question: A plane crashed, and every single person on board this flight was killed, yet there were survivors. Explain how?
results.correct_answer: The married people lived and the singles died.
results.model_answer: The married people lived and the singles died.

results.question: One lady ordered a fast delivery pizza and her order came the next year. How is this possible?
results.correct_answer: She ordered the pizza for New Year's Day.
results.model_answer: She ordered the pizza for New Year's Day.

results.question: In Jamaica, if you drop a steel ball weighing five pounds from a height of 45 inches, it falls more rapidly through the water at 40 degrees Fahrenheit than water at 20 degrees Fahrenheit. How is that possible?
results.correct_answer: At 20 degrees Fahrenheit the water would be ice.
results.model_answer: At 20 degrees Fahrenheit the water would be ice.

results.question: Danny was driving his semi under an overpass when suddenly he came to a screening halt. Danny wasn't paying enough attention and inadvertently drove under the overpass that was just barely as high as his truck. The semi was wedge so tightly that he could not go forward or backward. A fellow tracker came by and told him how he could easily get the semi out from under the bridge. What did he suggest?
results.correct_answer: He told Danny to let some air out of his tires.
results.model_answer: He told Danny to let some air out of his tires.

results.question: A boy had just gotten out of the shower and was getting ready for his prom, shaved and with cologne, and there would be an after-party. And his mom and dad said to be home for the next sunrise and he was home for the next sunrise but with a full-grown beard. How can this be?
results.correct_answer: He lives in Alaska and sunrise are every six months.
results.model_answer: He lives in Alaska and sunrise are every six months.

results.question: Why is it so cold on Christmas?
results.correct_answer: Because it's in December.

results.question: Wednesday,Tom and Joe went to a restaurant and ate dinner. When they were done they paid and left. Tom and Joe didn't pay. Who did?
results.correct_answer: Wednesday. The name of the third person in the group, not the day.
results.model_answer: Wednesday. The name of the third person in the group, not the day.

results.question: A magician had a water glass that was filled to the top. Holding the glass above his head he let it drop to the carpet without spilling a single drop of water. How could he manage to drop the glass from a height of six feet and not spill any water?
results.correct_answer: The glass was filled, but not with water.
results.model_answer: The glass was filled, but not with water.

results.question: Craig died in Florida. Shortly after, Tracy died at sea. Nobody mourned, In fact, everyone was absolute delighted. Why?
results.correct_answer: They were both hurricanes.

results.question: Morgan was making apricot jam. She put all the apricots in the pot and stirred them up. Then she remembered she had to add 1 ounce of lemon juice for every two apricots! How did she figure out how much lemon juice to put?
results.correct_answer: She counted the pits of apricots.
results.model_answer: She counted the pits of apricots.

results.question: A woman who lives in new york legally married three men, she did not get divorce, get an enrollment, or legally seperate. How is this possible?
results.correct_answer: She is a minister.

results.question: George was cleaning the windows on the eighteenth floor of an office block when there was a massive power failure. The electric hoist on his platform was immoblized. So how did he manage to get down before the power was restored?
results.correct_answer: George walked down the stairs. He was cleaning the inside of the windows.
results.model_answer: George walked down the stairs. He was cleaning the inside of the windows.

results.question: A man buys a rope from a woman for $3.00 and hands the woman a $10 bill. The woman goes into the grocery store next door to get change. She returns and gives the man $7.00. After the man leaves, the clerk from the store comes and says, "Hey, that was a counterfeit bill you gave me." The woman gives the clerk a good bill. How much has the woman lost?
results.correct_answer: Seven dollars plus the rope.

results.question: Two camels were facing in opposite directions. One was facing due East and one was facing due West. They were in the desert so there was no reflection. How can they manage to see each other without walking around or turning around or moving their heads?
results.correct_answer: The two camels were facing each other the entire time.
results.model_answer: The two camels were facing each other the entire time.

results.question: A sharpshooter hung up his hat and put on a blindfolded. He then walked 100 yards, turned around, and shot a bullet through his hat. The blindfolded was a perfectly good one, completely blocking the man's vision. How did he manage this?
results.correct_answer: He hung his hat on the barrel of his gun.
results.model_answer: He hung his hat on the barrel of his gun.

results.question: Two cops walked into a room with no windows and found a dead man who obviously hung himself from the ceiling, though they couldn't figure out how. There was no chair beneath him that he might have jumped off of, or a table. Just a paddle of water. How'd he do it?
results.correct_answer: He stood on a block of ice until it melted.
results.model_answer: He stood on a block of ice until it melted.

results.question: A truck driver went three blocks the wrong way down a one-way street without breaking the law. How come?
results.correct_answer: The truck driver was walking.

results.question: Nolan is outside a shop. She can not read the signs but she knows she needs to go in to make a purchase. What store is she at?
results.correct_answer: Eye glasses store.

results.question: Which would see most clearly in total darkness? A bat, a tiger, or an owl.
results.correct_answer: None of above.

results.question: There was a man who everybody called Batman he knew nothing about bats and thought they were gross. He still loved it when people called him Batman! How could this be?
results.correct_answer: He was the star baseball player.
results.model_answer: He was the star baseball player.

results.question: A woman went to visit her bank manager and she took her young daughter with her. The bank manager said that the woman's daughter could stay with his secretary during the meeting. When the woman and her daughter left, the secretary turned to the other secretary and said to her, "That little girl was my daughter." How could that be?
results.correct_answer: The secretary was the girl's father.

results.question: Maria and Juliana went out for drinks together. They ordered the same drink. Juliana was really thirsty and finished five in the time it took Maria to finish one. The drinks were poisoned, but only Maria died. How?
results.correct_answer: The poison was in the ice.
results.model_answer: The poison was in the ice.

results.question: I left my campsite and hired south for 3 miles. Then I turned east and hired for 3 miles. I then turned north and hired for 3 miles, at which time I came upon a bear inside my tent eating my food! What color was the bear?
results.correct_answer: White.

results.question: How can a man who shaves several times a day still sport a long beard?
results.correct_answer: He's a barber.

results.question: A rancher had 500 cows and took one shot that got them all. How did he do it?
results.correct_answer: A panoramic photo.

results.question: A man dies of old age on his 25 birthday. How is this possible?
results.correct_answer: He was born on February 29th

results.question: Mrs. Adams tells her neighbor that her son turns 16 today and that next birthday he'll be 20. How can that be?
results.correct_answer: He was born on February 29.

results.question: A man stands on one side of a river, his dog on the other. The man calls his dog, who immediately crosses the river without getting wet and without using a bridge or a boat. How did the dog do it?
results.correct_answer: The river was frozen.

results.question: A woman is sitting in her hotel room and hears a knock at the door. She opens the door to see a man whom she's never met before. He says, "I'm sorry, I have made a mistake, I thought this was my room." He then goes down the corridor and into the elevator. The woman goes back into her room and calls security. What made the woman so suspicious of the man?
results.correct_answer: If he really thought it was his hotel room, he would have tried to open the door not knock on it first.
results.model_answer: If he really thought it was his hotel room, he would have tried to open the door not knock on it first.

results.question: In a one-story house at the corner of the road, the bedrooms were yellow, the kitchen was orange, the living room was red, the garage was blue, the entry hall was green and the sitting room was purple. What color were the stairs?
results.correct_answer: None of above.

results.question: Sally lives in a place where six months of the year is mild summer and the temperature drops significantly the other six months. She owns a lake where there is a small island. She wants to build a house on the island and needs to get materials there. She doesn't have a boat, plane, or anything to transport them to the island. How does Sally solve this problem?
results.correct_answer: She waits to take the materials over during the colder months because the lake will freeze over, so she can walk over it.
results.model_answer: She waits to take the materials over during the colder months because the lake will freeze over, so she can walk over it.

results.question: What word would you use to describe a man who does not have all his fingers on one hand?
results.correct_answer: None of above.

results.question: How did the boy kick his soccer ball ten feet, and then have it come back to him on its own?
results.correct_answer: He kicked it up.

results.question: Two mothers and two daughters went out to eat, everyone ate one slice of pizza, yet only three slices were eaten. How's that possible?
results.correct_answer: The group included a grandmother, her daughter and her daughter's daughter.
results.model_answer: The group included a grandmother, her daughter and her daughter's daughter.

results.question: A man dressed in all black is walking down a country lane. Suddenly, a large black car with no lights on comes around the corner and speeches to a halt. How did the car's driver know he was there?
results.correct_answer: It was day time.
results.model_answer: The driver is good at listening.

results.question: Why are 1968 pennies worth more than 1967 pennies?
results.correct_answer: Because there is one more penny in 1968 pennies than in 1967 pennies

results.question: You are driving a bus. When you begin your route, there is an old woman named Mrs. Smith and a young boy named Raymond are on the bus. At the first stop, the old woman leaves, and a salesman, named Ed, enters. At the next stop, Jack and his sister Jill get on, as well as three women with shopping bags. The bus travels fifteen minutes, then stops and Raymond gets off and a man and his wife get on. Next, a woman with a bird in a cage gets on the bus. Who is bus driver?
results.correct_answer: None of above.

results.question: How much dirt is there in a hole that is 3 feet deep, 6 feet long, and 4 feet wide?
results.correct_answer: None of above.

results.question: How can you lift an elephant with one hand?
results.correct_answer: None of above.

results.question: Name the most recent year in which New Year's came before Christmas.
results.correct_answer: This year.

results.question: If the vice president were to die, who is supposed to be president?
results.correct_answer: The President.

results.question: Some months have eight letters in their name, whereas others have five. How many have three?
results.correct_answer: They all do.

results.question: Sandy claims she saw an ocean without water, and it turns out, she was being honest. How is that possible?
results.correct_answer: The ocean is on a map.
results.model_answer: The ocean is on a map.

results.question: While a cat was outside, it started to downpour. The cat couldn’t find any shelter and got completely soaked by the rain, yet not a single hair was wet. How could this be?
results.correct_answer: It was a hairless cat.

results.question: Two men had to go to the south side of the river, the river is pretty wide and rapid. There is one boat, but only one person fits on it. Lucky they both got to the south side of the river. How?
results.correct_answer: One person already at the south side of the river.
results.model_answer: One person already at the south side of the river.

results.question: A girl went over to the U.S. on the 4th of July and parties for 3 days and came back on the 4th of July. How did she do that?
results.correct_answer: She went over on the date of the 4th of July and came home on the boat called the July 4.

results.question: There are 8 brothers. Brother 1 is playing checkers. Brother 2 is eating. Brother 3 is watching video games. Brother 4 is dancing. Brother 5 is texting. Brother 6 is at school. Brother 7 is sleeping. What is Brother 8 doing?
results.correct_answer: Brother 8 is playing checkers.

results.question: There lived a man in a house and every time he went to bed he turned on the light. One night, the man forgot to turn on the light. The next day he read the paper and sinking into deep self-blame. Why?
results.correct_answer: The man lived in a lighthouse. He forgot to turn on the light and a ship crashed.
results.model_answer: The man lived in a lighthouse. He forgot to turn on the light and a ship crashed.

results.question: David's birthday is in December, but it is never in the winter. Why?
results.correct_answer: He lives in Australia.

results.question: Two teams were playing soccer against each other. One player from Team One scored a hat trick (triple goal). Their teammates were very happy. But when he scored the fourth goal, their teammates scolded him instead. How could this be possible?
results.correct_answer: The player scored in their own goal.
results.model_answer: The player scored in their own goal.

results.question: Two girls have the same parents and were born at the same hour of the same day of the same month, but they are not twins. How can this be possible?
results.correct_answer: They were not born in the same year.

results.question: A man is walking along the sea beach. To his surprise, he can clearly see his own footstep without turning back. How is that possible?
results.correct_answer: He is walking backward.
results.model_answer: The reflection by the sea let him saw the footsetp.

results.question: Three people are stuck inside a dark cave. The first fears the complete darkness, the second fears bats, and the third fears getting lost. They don't have any flashlights, lanterns or any kind of light source, and their cell phones have no signal so they can't use them as a light source either. How do they get out of the cave?
results.correct_answer: They can just walk out as it's daytime outside the cave.

results.question: A woman living in London, has four sons. All of them are doctors, but none of them ever attended medical school. How is this possible?
results.correct_answer: The woman's sons are all "doctors" in the academic sense
results.model_answer: The woman's sons are all "doctors" in the academic sense

results.question: A girl slipped and fell into a 60-foot well, yet she didn't get wet. How is this possible?
results.correct_answer: The well is dry.

results.question: I buried a stone in the ground, and it grew and produced fruit. How is this possible?
results.correct_answer: The stone was actually a seed, not an ordinary stone. The seed of a cherry is called a stone.
results.model_answer: The stone was actually a seed, not an ordinary stone. The seed of a cherry is called a stone.

results.question: An electric train is going south at 98 mph. The wind is blowing northeast. Which direction is the smoke blowing?
results.correct_answer: None of above.

results.question: There was a farmer who had a problem with his chicken coop, and he wanted to go check it out. When he got there he noticed that there was a rooster on top of the barn. Now the barn was shaped with a triangle-type top. But suddenly the rooster laid an egg. Which side did it roll on?
results.correct_answer: It didn't because rosters don't lay eggs.
results.model_answer: It didn't because rosters don't lay eggs.

results.question: There is a pink single-story house and everything in it is pink. The doors are pink, the windows are pink and the top is pink. What color are the stairs?
results.correct_answer: There are no stairs in a single story house.
results.model_answer: There are no stairs in a single story house.

results.question: Two fathers and two sons went fishing one day. They were there the whole day and only caught 3 fish. One father said, that is enough for all of us, we will have one each. How can this be possible?
results.correct_answer: There was the father, his son, and his son's son.
results.model_answer: There was the father, his son, and his son's son.

results.question: A bus driver was heading down a street in Colorado. He went right past a stop sign without stopping, he turned left where there was a "no left turn" sign, and he went the wrong way on a one-way street. Then he went on the left side of the road past a cop car. Still - he didn't break any traffic laws. Why not?
results.correct_answer: He was walking.
results.response:
The “bus driver” isn’t driving a bus at all. He’s simply a bus driver who is **walking** down the street. The traffic rules that forbid passing a stop sign, turning left where a “no left turn” sign is posted, driving the wrong way on a one‑way street, or walking on the left side of the road apply to vehicles, not pedestrians. Therefore, while walking he can do all of those things legally.

**Answer: A**

results.question: A pet shop owner had a parrot with a sign on its cage that said "Parrot repeats everything it hears". Davey bought the parrot and for two weeks he spoke to it and it didn't say a word. He returned the parrot but the shopkeeper said he never lied about the parrot. How can this be?
results.correct_answer: The parrot was deaf.

results.question: How can you leave a room with two legs and return with six legs?
results.correct_answer: Bring a chair back with you.
results.model_answer: Bring a chair back with you.

results.question: Some months have 30 days, some months have 31 days. How many have 28?
results.correct_answer: They all do.

results.question: Two students attend the same school, have the same teacher, and were born on the same day of the same month, but they are not siblings. How can this be possible?
results.correct_answer: They were not born in the same year.
results.model_answer: They were not born in the same year.

results.question: A man was just doing his job when his suit was torn. Why did he die three minutes later?
results.correct_answer: He was an astronaut on a space walk, doing repairs.

results.question: How can you physically stand behind your friend as he physically stands behind you?
results.correct_answer: By standing back to back.

results.question: All the electricity was out in town and none of the street lights or traffic signals had power. A dark limousine was cruising down the newly paved blacktop, with its headlights off. A young boy dressed totally in black (with no reflector) stepped out to cross the street. The moon wasn't out and the boy had no flashlight, yet the driver stopped to let the boy cross the street. How did the driver see the boy?
results.correct_answer: The driver saw the boy because it was during the daylight hours.
results.model_answer: The driver saw the boy because it was during the daylight hours.

results.question: Four cars come to a four way stop, all coming from a different direction. They can't decide who got there first, so they all go forward at the same time. They do not crash into each other, but all four cars go. How is this possible?
results.correct_answer: They all made right-hand turns.

results.question: A man was born in 1898. He is still alive now at the age 33  How is this possible ?
results.correct_answer: he was born in room 1898 in the hospital.
results.model_answer: he was born in room 1898 in the hospital.

results.question: A man is late for an appointment and, in his haste, takes a corner so fast that only two wheels were on the pavement. A policeman is standing on the corner, but he did not give him a ticket. Why?
results.correct_answer: He was on a motorcycle.

results.question: Four men sat down to play, They played all night till the break of day. They played for gold and not for fun, With separate scores for every one. When they had come to square accounts, They all had made quite fair amounts. Can you explain the paradox, If no one lost, how all could gain?
results.correct_answer: The men were musicians..

results.question: Imagine Johnny, a party clown, is carrying three pieces of gold each piece weighing one kilogram. While taking a walk he comes to a bridge that has a sign posted saying the bridge could hold only a maximum of 80 kilograms. John weighs 78 kilograms and the gold weighs three kilograms. Johnny reads the sign and still safely crossed the bridge with all the gold. How did he manage this?
results.correct_answer: Johnny is a clown so he has mastered juggling. When he came to the bridge he judged the gold, always keeping one piece in the air.
results.model_answer: Johnny is a clown so he has mastered juggling. When he came to the bridge he judged the gold, always keeping one piece in the air.

results.question: Monica took part in a furiously competitive race, which she won due to her speed and agility. Despite this, she was not allowed to collect the winning trophy. Why?
results.correct_answer: Monica is the name of the horse that won the race.
results.model_answer: Monica is the name of the horse that won the race.

results.question: A woman was horrified to find a fly in her tea. The waiter took her cup and went into the kitchen and returned with a fresh cup of tea. She shouted, "You brought me the same tea!" How did she know?
results.correct_answer: She had already put sugar in it and when she tasted the new tea it was already sweet.
results.model_answer: She had already put sugar in it and when she tasted the new tea it was already sweet.

results.question: In 1990, a person is 15 years old. In 1995 that same person is 10 years old. How is this possible?
results.correct_answer: The years are in B.C.

results.question: Bouncing Bob was riding a particularly risky horse when suddenly its bridge came off. As they raced down the road, a screaming Bob lung to the horse's ears for dear life. Out of the corner of his eye, Bob saw a car coming, and realizing the horse was completely out of control, he panicked. Failing his arms about, he accidentally caused the horse to come to an abrupt halt. What could Bouncing Bob have done to make the horse stop?
results.correct_answer: Bob accidentally put his hands over the horse's eyes. If a horse can't see he will automatically stop.

results.question: A large truck is crossing a bridge 1 mile long. The bridge can only hold 14000 lbs, which is the exact weight of the truck. The truck makes it half way across the bridge and stops. A bird lands on the truck, but the bridge doesn't collapse. How is that possible?
results.correct_answer: Because it has driven a half mile - you would subtract the gas used from the total weight of the truck.

results.question: A store has on sale a computer and word processor small enough to fit in your pocket. It can add, multiply, subtract, divide, and write in all languages. A delete device will correct any error. No electricity is required to operate it. The price? Only a few cents! How can the store make a profit by selling it so cheap?
results.correct_answer: The computer is a wooden pencil with an eraser.
results.model_answer: The computer is a wooden pencil with an eraser.

results.question: A man sails off on a cruise between Mexico and the USA. He does not stop at any ports and does not even come out of the cabin, yet he makes $300,000 from his trip. How?
results.correct_answer: He's a smuggler.

results.question: Two cars were involved in an accident in the center of town. The man who was driving a little green car, had overtaken a big black car. The driver had misjudged the distance between him and the on-coming traffic and had to serve back in, causing the black car to serve and crash into a shop window. When the occupants of the cars were examined everyone in the green car was okay, but in the black car was one dead man. However, the driver of the green car was not charged with manslaughter, why was this so?
results.correct_answer: The black car was a hearse and was on its way to a funeral.
results.model_answer: The black car was a hearse and was on its way to a funeral.

results.question: A horse was tied to a rope 5 meters long and the horses food was 15 meters away from the horse. How did the horse reach the food?
results.correct_answer: The rope wasn't tied to anything so he could reach the food.
results.model_answer: The rope wasn't tied to anything so he could reach the food.

results.question: A murderer is condemned to death. He has to choose between three rooms. The first is full of raging fires, the second is full of assassins with loaded guns, and the third is full of lions that haven't eaten in 3 years. Which room is safest for him?
results.correct_answer: The third. Lions that haven't eaten in three years are dead.

results.question: There are 2 cops parked along a one-way street looking for traffic violations. They spot a taxi driver going in the wrong direction, yet they do nothing. Why?
results.correct_answer: The taxi driver wasn't driving at the time, he was walking.
results.model_answer: The taxi driver wasn't driving at the time, he was walking.

results.question: There is a bus full of people travelling over San Francisco and no one gets off the bus throughout the journey. But when it gets to the other side there is not a single person left. How is this possible?
results.correct_answer: They are all married.

results.question: One day, a thirty-story building was on fire. A man jumped out a window and nothing happened to him. Nothing was under him. How is this possible?
results.correct_answer: He was first floor.

results.question: One morning a man is leaving on a business trip and finds he left some paperwork at his office. He runs into his office to get it and the night watchman stops him and says, "Sir, don't get on the plane. I had a dream last night that the plane would crash and everyone would die!" The man takes his word and cancels his trip. Sure enough, the plane crashes and everyone dies. The next morning the man gives the watchman a $1,000 reward for saving his life and then fires him. Why did he fire the watchman that saved his life?
results.correct_answer: The watchman was fired for sleeping on the job.

results.question: A man was looking through a 6th floor window,  thought for a minute then jumped,  but did not die or get hurt at all. How?
results.correct_answer: He jumped in the window. He was outside the window to wash it.

results.question: There are two bodies on the floor. They are surrounded by water and broken glass. How did they die?
results.correct_answer: The bodies were goldfish because their fishbowl got knocked over onto the floor and broke.
results.model_answer: The bodies were goldfish because their fishbowl got knocked over onto the floor and broke.

results.question: What question can someone ask all day long, always get completely different answers, and yet all the answers could be correct?
results.correct_answer: What time is it?

results.question:
Stranger: Does your dog bite? 
Farmer: No. 
All of a sudden the dog bites the stranger on the leg. Yet the farmer was telling the truth! How can this be?
results.correct_answer: It was not the farmer's dog.
results.model_answer: The dog never bit the farmer.

results.question: Why can't a woman, who is now living in Canada, not be buried in the USA?
results.correct_answer: Because she is still alive.

results.question: A man grabbed a woman's ring and pulled on it, then dropped it. How did this save her life?
results.correct_answer: They were skydiving, and she was unconscious.
results.model_answer: They were skydiving, and she was unconscious.

results.question: Two women and two doctors walk into an ice cream parlor. They each order an ice cream cone. When their ice cream come, there is only 1 strawberry,1 chocolate and 1 vanilla. How come they didn't complain?
results.correct_answer: One of the women was a doctor.
results.model_answer: One of the women was a doctor.

results.question: There are 50 horses and 50 kings riding along an old dirt road. They came to a peach tree with 50 peaches. Each took one, yet there were still 49 left. How is this possible?
results.correct_answer: Each is the name of one of the kings and he's the only one that took one.
results.model_answer: Each is the name of one of the kings and he's the only one that took one.

results.question: Fred is listening to the radio when it suddenly stops playing. Nobody is with Fred and nobody touches the radio. A few seconds later, the radio resumes playing. How can this be?
results.correct_answer: Fred was driving his car through a tunnel.
results.model_answer: Fred was driving his car through a tunnel.

results.question:
A man is discovered dead sitting at his desk, alone in the locked office.  He did not commit suicide and there was no weapons in the room. The only clue is a sealed envelope on the desk in front of him.
How did he die?
results.correct_answer: The envelope glue was poisoned and when the man linked the envelope to seal it, he died.

results.question: What question can never be answered with a Yes?
results.correct_answer: Are you asleep?

results.question: You're in a room and there is a ghost in the room, but you're the only one in the room. How is this possible?
results.correct_answer: You're the ghost.

results.question: How many gold coins can a leprechaun throw in an empty pot?
results.correct_answer: One. After that, it no longer empty.

results.question: Thomas was caught stealing 27 times in one year, yet he was never arrested. How come?
results.correct_answer: Thomas was a baseball player and was stealing bases.
results.model_answer: Thomas was a baseball player and was stealing bases.

results.question: Not a single parent objected when the teacher spanked every child in the class. How come?
results.correct_answer: The teacher was in an orphanage school.
results.model_answer: The teacher was in an orphanage school.

results.question: Cleopatra and Anthony were near a fish bowl and somehow they died. What happened?
results.correct_answer: Cleopatra and Anthony were fish and they can't survive without water.

results.question: There is a bomb on top of a computer. Around the computer there is a phone, a hairbrush, keys and a cup. When the explosion comes which item is destroyed first?
results.correct_answer: None of above.

results.question: Andy is put in a cell with a dirt floor and only one window. The window is too high for him to reach. The only thing in the cell is a shovel. He won't be able to get any food or water and only has two days to escape or he'll die. Andy can't dig a tunnel because it will take him much longer than two days to do it. How will Andy escape from the cell?
results.correct_answer: Andy has to use the shovel to create a pile of dirt under the window so he can climb up onto it and escape from the cell.
results.model_answer: Andy has to use the shovel to create a pile of dirt under the window so he can climb up onto it and escape from the cell.

results.question: An elevator is on the ground floor. There are four people in the elevator including me. When the lift reaches the first floor, one person gets out and three people get in. The lift goes up to the second floor, 2 people get out, 6 people get in. It then goes up to the next floor up, no one gets out but 12 people get in. Halfway up to the next floor up the elevator cable snaps, it crashes to the floor. Everyone else dies in the elevator except me. How did I survive?
results.correct_answer: I got off on the first floor.
results.model_answer: I got off on the first floor.

results.question: Two boxers are in a match scheduled for 12 rounds. (Pure boxing only. There are no kicking or takedown). One of the boxers gets knocked out after only six rounds, yet no man throws a punch. How is this possible?
results.correct_answer: Both the boxers were female.

results.question: I have a large money box, 10 inches wide and 5 inches tall.  Roughly how many coins can I place until my money box is no longer empty?
results.correct_answer: One.

results.question: During which month do people sleep the least?
results.correct_answer: February.

results.question: Laura says that she can throw a ball so that it flies away from her, will change direction suddenly, and then come right back to her all by itself. Is that even possible?
results.correct_answer: Yes, if she throws it straight in the air.
results.model_answer: Yes, if she throws it straight in the air.

results.question: A man pushes his car until he reaches a hotel. When he arrives, he realizes he's bankrupt. What happened?
results.correct_answer: He's playing Monopoly and he lands on a space with a hotel and doesn't have the money to pay the fee.
results.model_answer: He's playing Monopoly and he lands on a space with a hotel and doesn't have the money to pay the fee.

results.question: A woman enters a room and presses a button. Within seconds she instantly loses 20 pounds. How did she lose the weight?
results.correct_answer: The room is actually an elevator. When she gets inside to go down, the elevator accelerates downward, making her weight temporarily lower.

results.question: A woman opened a door, streamed, and then was found dead a few minutes later. No gunshot were heard in the area. What happened to her?
results.correct_answer: She was on an airplane and opened the door while the plane was in flight, falling to her death.

results.question: Sam's dad couldn't keep his son from playing video games. To keep Sam from playing video games all the time, the dad grabbed a hammer and solved the problem. Now he can play video games but Sam cannot. What did the dad do
results.correct_answer: The dad built a shelf out of Sam's reach and put the video game console up there. The dad can still reach it to play but Sam cannot.

results.question: Every two weeks a woman sits down and writes two words on 60 sheets of paper. Why does she do this?
results.correct_answer: The woman owns her own business with 60 employees. Every week she signs her name on their paychecks.
results.response:
The puzzle is meant to make you look for a clever, everyday situation rather than a literal “pay‑check” or “hand‑writing practice.”  
Every two weeks the woman has 60 papers in front of her – for example, a class of 60 students handing in assignments. On each sheet she simply writes the two‑word comment **“Well done.”**  

That explains why she writes exactly two words on each of 60 sheets every two weeks, and none of the given choices (hand‑writing practice, signing paychecks, “taking full advantage of papers”) matches this situation.

**Answer: D**.

results.question: A man is in a grocery store and fills a shopping cart. He walks out of the store without paying but no one stops him and no one calls the police. Why?
results.correct_answer: The man is an employee of the store and was filling the cart with trash to take out to the trash can.

results.question: If you were alone in a dark room, with only one match and an oil lamp, a fireplace, and a candle to choose from, which would you light first?
results.correct_answer: None of above.

results.question: How could a man go outside in the pouring rain without protection, and not have a hair on his head get wet?
results.correct_answer: He is bald.

results.question: If a monkey, a squirrel, and a bird are racing to the top of a coconut tree, who will get the banana first?
results.correct_answer: None of above.

results.question: A group of ten ostriches are in a field. If four of them take off flying, how many are left in the field?
results.correct_answer: None of above.

results.question: Two people were playing chess and both won. How did this happen?
results.correct_answer: They were playing against other opponents.
results.model_answer: They were playing against other opponents.

results.question: A hiker arrives near a lake. He sees a sign where it is written "No swimming allowed.", but the duck jumps into the water. Why?
results.correct_answer: Because ducks can't read.

results.question: Twenty-seven ducks are going to the pond. Five of them got lost, thirteen of them are staying home, and nine of them are at the pond. Where are the rest of them?
results.correct_answer: None of above.

results.question:
Ten fish are in a tank. Two fish drown. Four fish swim away.Three fish die.
How many fish are left in the tank?
results.correct_answer: None of above.

results.question: A woman went to Virginia to get a job. She promised her mother she would come back and visit sometime. Then, in the middle of winter, her mother shouted, "April is here!". How is this possible?
results.correct_answer: The woman's name is April.

results.question: What animal has no wings, but yet will fly?
results.correct_answer: A caterpillar.

results.question: A logical with some time to kill in a small town decided to get a haircut. The town had only two barber, each with his own shop. The logical glance into one shop and saw that it was extremely until. The barber needed a shave, his clothes were unkempt, and his hair was badly cut. The other shop was extremely neat. The barber was freshly shaved and spotlessly dressed, his hair neatly trimmed. Why did the logical return to the first shop for his haircut?
results.correct_answer: Each barber must have cut the other's hair. The logical picked the barber who had given his rival the better haircut.
results.model_answer: Each barber must have cut the other's hair. The logical picked the barber who had given his rival the better haircut.

results.question: A plane crashed between the border of Canada and America. Where do you bury the survivors?
results.correct_answer: None of above.

results.question: Imagine you are in a room, with no doors, windows, or anything. How do you get out?
results.correct_answer: Stop imagining.

results.question: I buried a stone in the ground, and it grew and produced fruit. How is this possible?
results.correct_answer: The stone was actually a seed, not an ordinary stone. The seed of a cherry is called a stone.
results.model_answer: You buried a stone in the ground, and as time passed, a tree grew above it. The fruit produced by the tree is unrelated to the stone itself.

results.question: How many birth days does the average person have?
results.correct_answer: They technically only have one birth day in their lifetime.
results.model_answer: They technically only have one birth day in their lifetime.

results.question: An electric train is going south at 98 mph. The wind is blowing northeast. Which direction is the smoke blowing?
results.correct_answer: There is no smoke. it's an electric train!
results.model_answer: There is no smoke. it's an electric train!

results.question: There is a pink single-story house and everything in it is pink. The doors are pink, the windows are pink and the top is pink. What color are the stairs?
results.correct_answer: There are no stairs in a single story house.
results.model_answer: There are no stairs in a single story house.

results.question: Ten pears hanging high, Ten men come passing by, Each took a pear and left nine hanging there. How could that be?
results.correct_answer: EACH is the name of one of the men.

results.question: The more you take, the more you leave behind
results.correct_answer: Footsteps.
